economic_notes: Canoe Lake Resource Development Inc. is a wholly-owned business enterprise accounted for under modified equity method. First Nation has an outstanding treaty benefits claim against the Government of Canada financed through RBC bank loans with insurance coverage. COVID-19 impacted gaming revenues and office hours but ISC increased funding to compensate.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2021 (statements signed April 12, 2022 — significantly delayed filing). Auditor issued emphasis of matter paragraph regarding outstanding treaty benefits claim against Government of Canada financed by RBC loan ($1,242,661 drawn). One GBE (Canoe Lake Resource Development Inc.) accounted for by modified equity method recorded a loss of $449,661. Capital lease obligations of $955,874 relate to two Brandt Tractor leases with corresponding sales-type lease receivables from the GBE. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($16,066,649) + MLTC Program Services ($3,386,139) + Meadow Lake Health & Social Development Authority ($3,311,427) + CMHC ($464,782) + MLTC Child & Family Services ($286,789) + Saskatchewan Rivers School Division ($10,000) + Government of Saskatchewan ($500) + First Nations Trust ($545,167) = $24,071,453; OSR calculated as total revenue minus total government transfers. Long-term debt covenant non-compliance noted but management does not expect penalties. Capital expenses segment ($4,810,155) includes housing and capital construction. First Nation Programs segment ($1,707,602) includes other band activities. No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text.
